core_mechanism: Inverse reconstruction of a latent interaction/coupling network from the observed activation (arrival) times of a spreading/cascade process, by selecting the edge set that maximizes the likelihood of the observed timings over all unobserved propagation trees, solved through a greedy submodular approximation with a provable near-optimality guarantee
brief_reason: The abstract recovers an unobserved network by finding the network that best explains the times at which nodes become infected or adopt information, without observing individual transmissions, and approximates the NP-hard likelihood optimum with a provably near-optimal efficient algorithm.
